title: haplocoelum foliolosum subsp. foliolosum
intro: Haplocoelum foliolosum subsp. foliolosum, also known as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, is a shrub or small tree that is native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Haplocoelum foliolosum subsp. foliolosum include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as a digestive aid and anti-diarrheal agent. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in various herbal preparations, such as syrups and ointments.
